summary:
Prostate cancer primarily develops in the peripheral zone due to its high frequency at around 70-80%, while it rarely occurs in other regions such as the central zone. This distribution pattern supports identifying the peripheral zone as the most commonly affected area by prostate carcinomas. The final choice aligns well based on these critical insights into tumor locations within prostatic tissue structures. To reiterate succinctly:

Prostate tumors frequently appear in the peripheral zones; they are rare in others including centrals ones indicating a strong association between malignancy and periphery areas specifically. Key point here being predominant occurrence noted clinically across multiple investigations confirming earlier assessments accurately pinpointing where majority arise from anatomically significant sites. 

This summary captures essential aspects regarding site-specific incidences crucial when evaluating options related directly towards understanding primary origins or risk factors associated more prominently among different sections composing overall glandular architecture pertinent medically speaking during diagnostic procedures involving biopsies etc...
